reasoning: The resolution criteria require the Project 2025 objective to "terminate the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program" to be marked as complete on the Keep Track spreadsheet before July 1, 2025. As of May 2025, the tracker does not mark this objective as complete. Since PSLF is established by statute, terminating it requires congressional action. Although Trump issued a March 2025 executive order limiting some employer eligibility, this did not terminate the program. While standalone bills limiting PSLF exist, the primary fast-tracked legislative vehicle (the fiscal budget reconciliation bill) advanced by the House Education Committee in early May 2025 explicitly declined to fully repeal the program, instead opting for narrower modifications (such as excluding medical/dental residencies). With only 1.5 months until the deadline, the slow pace of Congress combined with the fact that current significant legislation does not achieve full termination makes it highly unlikely the spreadsheet will mark this specific objective as complete before July 1.
